_meta.purpose: Competitor gap-analysis directives auto-injected into Zevo concept generation. Distilled from a fresh Meta Ad Library scrape (fitness/running/sports + nutrition). Tells the concept model what the category currently rewards, what is saturated/fatigued, and the benchmarks to hit. Re-generate after each market scrape.
_meta.sample: 99 freshly analyzed Meta ads, June 2026 — 48 fitness/running/sports + 51 nutrition/diet
sources: output/market-fitness/analyses.json (48 ads: AI fitness/workout/running/form apps, Strava, Freeletics, Whoop, Future, Fitbod, NTC)
output/market-nutrition/analyses.json (51 ads: MyFitnessPal, Noom, Lose It, Bevel, calorie/macro/AI-diet trackers, TR kalori/diyet apps)
Hook mix: problem 52%, curiosity 19%, demo 7%, social_proof 4%, stat_drop 3%, before_after 2%, shock 1%
CTA: 91% outro placement; dominant CTAs 'Install Now' (34) and 'Learn More' (26), usually paired with a free/cheap entry offer
Durations: median 32s; clusters at 11-20s, 21-30s, 31-45s, plus a large long-form/testimonial tail (45s+)

add: Open with an instantly relatable PROBLEM beat even when using a fancy hook technique — 52% of the category opens problem-first because it works. The hook style can be contrarian/curiosity/pattern-interrupt, but shot 0 must make the viewer feel a specific pain they recognize in <3s.

Lead with Zevo's ONE demonstrable mechanism (real-time, per-rep AI form correction) instead of a generic 'AI-powered' claim. The whole category screams 'AI' — our edge is that ours is visible and specific. Show the thing, don't just name it.

Add a credibility / social-proof beat (testimonial tone, a number, a before→after, or a 'binlerce kişi' style signal). Competitors lean hard on authority + transformation stories; Zevo concepts currently skip this almost entirely.

Use an 'all-in-one / tek uygulama' framing against fragmentation — competitors sell 'stop juggling 5 apps'. Zevo combines antrenman + form düzeltme + beslenme + ilerleme; say it explicitly in one breath.

End on a concrete, barrier-lowering CTA spoken in the outro voiceover (e.g. 'Bugün ücretsiz dene', 'Kart gerekmiyor') — NOT a CTA card. 91% of the category puts a low-friction install CTA at the very end; we removed the card but should keep the spoken low-friction nudge.
Quantify wherever possible — a timeframe, a count, a metric ('30 saniyede plan', '90 günde', 'her tekrarı'). Specific numbers beat vague aspiration and match what's converting now.
For donusum/kurucu tips, allow a longer ~30s storytelling/transformation arc — the category's winning emotional ads run 30-45s, longer than our default 20s, and need room for the arc to land.
remove: Generic 'AI-powered app solves your problem' framing with no on-screen proof — this is the single most saturated message in the category (the majority of the 99 ads). If we sound like everyone else in the first 3s, we lose.
Vague aspirational fluff ('forma gir', 'kendini daha iyi hisset') with no specific pain, number, or visible mechanism — the winning ads are concrete; the generic ones blend in.
Opening on the app UI or the feature name (already a hook rule; re-confirmed — zero top performers open on a product screen).
Burying the hook behind setup — keep shot 0 a pure pattern-interrupt/problem; never spend the first 3s on logo, slow intro, or context.
Long undifferentiated feature lists read like a spec sheet — competitors that list metrics still wrap them in a benefit. List at most 3, each tied to an outcome.
benchmarks: First on-screen subtitle <= 7 words; spoken hook 8-14 words; relatable pain landed within 3 seconds.
Total duration: 20s default for klasik/motivasyon/pattern-interrupt; allow up to ~30s for donusum/kurucu storytelling.
Exactly one concrete outro CTA with a low-friction offer, spoken (no card).
At least one credibility signal per concept (number, before/after, or testimonial-tone line).
Diversify hooks across concepts — do NOT collapse to plain 'problem' just because it's the category default; out-execute the 52% with sharper, specific openers.
